title: Narthana Vallabeswarar temple
tagline: Narthana Vallabeswarar temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Narthana Vallabeswarar Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located in Thirukoodalaiyathoor, a village in Cuddalore district in the South Indian state of Tamil Na
overview.description: Narthana Vallabeswarar Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located in Thirukoodalaiyathoor, a village in Cuddalore district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Shiva is worshipped as Narthana Vallabeswarar, and is represented by the lingam. His consort Parvati is depicted as Gnanasakthi and Parasakthi. The presiding deity is revered in the seventh-century Tamil Saiva canonical work, the Tevaram, written by Tamil saint poets known as the Nayanars and classified as Paadal Petra Sthalam.

topPlaces.description: The Manimuktha River is a river in the Kallakurichi and Cuddalore districts of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Varadarajanpettai
topPlaces.description: Varadarajanpet is a panchayat town in Ariyalur district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Bhu Varaha Swamy temple
topPlaces.description: The Bhu Varaha Swamy temple is a Hindu temple, located at Srimushnam, in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Constructed in the South Indian style of architecture, the temple is dedicated to Varaha (Bhu Varaha Swamy), the boar-avatar of the god Vishnu and his consort Lakshmi as Ambujavalli Thayar.

topPlaces.name: Alapakkam, Cuddalore
topPlaces.description: Alapakkam also called is a village in Cuddalore district in Tamil Nadu. It comes under Cuddalore taluk and Alappakkam and poovanikuppam village panchayat.

topPlaces.name: Srimushnam
topPlaces.description: Srimushnam is a town panchayat, and the headquarters of Srimushnam taluk, in Cuddalore district, Tamil Nadu, India. The town is sacred to Hindus and Buddhists.

topPlaces.name: Sethiyur
topPlaces.description: Sethiyur is a village in Cuddalore district, Tamil Nadu, on the south-west coast of India. It has a population of 1,347.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Narthana Vallabeswarar temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Narthana Vallabeswarar temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Narthana Vallabeswarar temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (111 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (110 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Narthana Vallabeswarar temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Narthana Vallabeswarar temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Narthana Vallabeswarar temple?
faq.a: Stays in Narthana Vallabeswarar temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Narthana Vallabeswarar temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Manimuktha River, Varadarajanpettai, Bhu Varaha Swamy temple, Alapakkam, Cuddalore, Srimushnam.
